steps.title: Clear the denominator
steps.content: Start with $$\frac{55}{x+6}=x.$$ Since the positive solution will not make $x+6=0$, multiply both sides by $x+6$: $$55=x(x+6).$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content: Rewrite as $$x^2+6x-55=0.$$ The numbers $11$ and $-5$ multiply to $-55$ and add to $6$, so $$(x+11)(x-5)=0.$$

steps.title: Choose the positive solution
steps.content: The solutions are $x=-11$ and $x=5$. The positive solution is 5. Graphing $y=55/(x+6)$ and $y=x$ shows the positive intersection at $x=5$.
